what_it_does: This CSS file re-points prominent UI affordances that were hard-coded to literal gold gradients so they read derived CSS custom accent properties instead, letting a JS-computed hue recolor the whole gold system together.
observations: Uses CSS custom properties with literal gold fallbacks for every gradient stop.
Targets buttons, send circles, the Vi orb, the Vi badge, the top-bar brand mark, the welcome orb, the next-step icon, and the active journey phase icon.
Includes a single tightly-scoped !important rule for the top-bar brand mark to override inline FlipBridge layout styles.
Loaded last so these declarations win over base component styles.
Mixes linear and radial gradients; some stops use --accent-grad-hi/--accent-grad-lo, others use --accent-orb-hi.
Comments explicitly name the affordances being remapped and why.
standouts: Surgical override layer rather than editing each component stylesheet.
The !important on .topbar__brand [data-flip='vi'] to beat inline background styles.
Every gradient stop carries a fallback literal gold color.
The file is framed as a 'loaded last' recolor shim.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties with fallback values for progressive recoloring.
Documents intent and rationale in block and inline comments.
Overrides are grouped by visual system (buttons, orb, badge, brand, welcome, next-step, journey).
concerns: !important is used, which can complicate future overrides.
All recoloring depends on JS setting the custom properties; fallbacks may look correct but hide missing-token failures.
No dark-mode, high-contrast, or accessibility notes are present.
The comments reference FlipBridge travelers without defining them in this file.
notes: A final-layer accent-recolor shim that swaps literal gold gradients to derived accent tokens across the key branded affordances.
questions: How does the JS compute the hue and populate these tokens?
What is a FlipBridge traveler and where is its inline style set?
Is this file guaranteed to be loaded last in the build?
Are there other hard-coded gold affordances not covered here?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it remaps the listed hard-coded gold gradients to derived accent tokens and is positioned to override base styles.
apparent_themes: Surgical override rather than component rewrite
intention_signals: The file is trying to let a single JS hue change recolor every remaining gold-branded affordance without touching individual component source files, by layering a small override sheet at the end of the cascade.
novelty: It treats recoloring as a separate 'theme' layer loaded after components, including a FlipBridge-aware !important escape hatch for an inline-styled brand mark.
contribution: Provides the mapping that converts hard-coded gold gradients on high-visibility affordances into dynamic accent-token references, enabling centralized hue changes while leaving component stylesheets untouched.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS override file that remaps hard-coded gold gradients on primary buttons, send icons, the Vi orb and badge, the top-bar brand mark, the welcome orb, the next-step icon, and the active journey-phase icon to derived CSS custom accent properties.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to be loaded last so a JS-computed accent hue automatically recolors the remaining hard-coded gold affordances across the UI.
